Tool_section_80d_Description: Health insurance premium tax benefit. Self + parents deduction up to ₹1 lakh.
Tool_section_80d_LearnText: Section 80D lets you claim a deduction on health insurance premiums for yourself, your family, and your parents under the old tax regime. Limits rise when you or your parents are senior citizens. Preventive health check-ups (up to ₹5,000) count inside the self/family limit — not on top of it.
Tool_section_80d_FormulaNote: Illustrative old-regime limits: self/family ₹25,000 (₹50,000 if senior); parents ₹25,000 (₹50,000 if senior parents). Preventive checkup ≤ ₹5,000 within the self limit. Estimated tax saving ≈ eligible × your marginal slab. New regime generally does not allow 80D — confirm current rules.
Tool_section_80d_SelfHint: Premium for self, spouse, and dependent children.
Tool_section_80d_LabelSelfSenior: Self / family includes a senior citizen (60+)
Tool_section_80d_PreventiveHint: Capped at ₹5,000 and counted inside the self/family limit.
Tool_section_80d_ExampleStep_1: Self/family limit = ₹25,000 (not senior). Premium ₹20,000 + preventive ₹5,000 = ₹25,000 → eligible ₹25,000.
Tool_section_80d_ExampleStep_2: Parents are senior → limit ₹50,000. Premium ₹30,000 → eligible ₹30,000.
Tool_section_80d_ExampleStep_5: If self were also senior, the self limit would rise to ₹50,000.
Tool_section_80d_Step_1: Apply the self/family limit (₹25k or ₹50k if senior).
Tool_section_80d_Step_2: Add preventive checkup up to ₹5k inside that self limit.
Tool_section_80d_Step_3: Apply the separate parents limit (₹25k or ₹50k if senior).
Tool_section_80d_Step_4: Estimate tax saving as eligible × your slab.
Tool_section_80d_Info_Purpose: Estimate eligible health-insurance deduction and tax saving under Section 80D.
Tool_section_80d_Info_WhyKnow: Self and parents have separate caps; senior status and preventive checkups change how much you can claim.
Tool_section_80d_Info_RealLife: Buying family floater cover, covering parents’ premiums, claiming annual health check-ups.
Tool_section_80d_Info_Goal_3: Estimate tax saving at your slab
Tool_section_80d_Info_Alt_80c_When: When planning PPF, ELSS, NSC, and other ₹1.5L investments.
Tool_section_80d_Info_Alt_Hra_When: When you pay rent and receive HRA.
Tool_section_80d_Info_Alt_Tax_When: Full-year liability including salary and other income.
Tool_section_80d_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra separates self and parents buckets, senior limits, and preventive checkup inside the self cap.
Tool_section_80d_Info_Motto: Insure the family — claim what the law allows.
Tool_nsc_Description: National Savings Certificate maturity at ~7.7%. 5-year lock-in with 80C benefit.
Tool_nsc_LearnText: National Savings Certificate (NSC) is a government savings product with a fixed 5-year tenure and annual compounding. The purchase amount qualifies under Section 80C (within the overall ₹1.5 lakh cap). Interest is reinvested; in a simplified view, accrued interest in intermediate years may also qualify for 80C.
Tool_nsc_FormulaNote: Maturity = P × (1 + r)^5 with annual compounding. Default rate ~7.7% is illustrative and changes with government notifications. Interest is taxable; 80C treatment of reinvested interest is simplified here — confirm with a tax professional.
Tool_nsc_DepositHint: Purchase amount; counts toward Section 80C (overall ₹1.5L cap).
Tool_nsc_TenureHint: NSC VIII Issue tenure is 5 years.
Tool_nsc_ExampleStep_3: Total interest ≈ ₹44,900, credited and reinvested each year.
Tool_nsc_ExampleStep_5: Interest in years 1–4 is typically treated as reinvested and may qualify for 80C in those years (simplified).
Tool_nsc_Step_2: Apply annual compounding for 5 years: P × (1+r)^5.
Tool_nsc_Step_3: List year-wise interest on the growing balance.
Tool_nsc_Step_4: Note 80C on deposit and simplified intermediate interest.
Tool_nsc_Info_Purpose: Project 5-year NSC maturity and year-wise reinvested interest.
Tool_nsc_Info_WhyKnow: NSC is a popular 80C product with a fixed lock-in; knowing maturity and interest helps compare with PPF and tax-saving FDs.
Tool_nsc_Info_RealLife: Parking a lump sum for 5 years, filling remaining 80C room, gifting NSC to family.
Tool_nsc_Info_Alt_Ppf_Pros: EEE tax treatment; flexible yearly deposits.
Tool_nsc_Info_Alt_Fd_When: When you prefer a bank 5-year lock-in FD.
Tool_nsc_Info_Alt_Fd_Pros: Easy to open at your bank.
Tool_nsc_Info_Alt_Fd_Cons: Interest taxable; rate varies by bank.
Tool_nsc_Info_Alt_80c_When: When combining NSC with PPF, ELSS, and other 80C items.
Tool_nsc_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows NSC maturity, year-wise reinvested interest, and a simplified 80C note in one view.
Tool_nsc_Info_Motto: Sovereign savings with a clear 5-year path.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_Description: 5-year tax-saver FD under 80C. Compare bank rates and lock-in returns.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_LearnText: A tax-saving fixed deposit has a mandatory 5-year lock-in and qualifies for Section 80C up to ₹1.5 lakh (old regime, within the overall 80C cap). Interest is taxable. Choose quarterly or annual compounding to see maturity and an estimated tax saving at your slab.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_FormulaNote: Maturity uses A = P × (1 + r/n)^(n×5). 80C eligible = min(deposit, ₹1,50,000). Tax saving ≈ eligible × slab. Interest remains taxable; new regime generally does not allow 80C.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_DepositHint: 80C benefit capped at {0} (overall 80C limit shared with other instruments).
Tool_tax_saving_fd_LockinHint: Tax-saver FDs have a mandatory 5-year lock-in.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_ExampleStep_1: Deposit ₹1,50,000 in a 5-year tax-saver FD at 7%.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_ExampleStep_3: Full ₹1.5 lakh counts under 80C (if room remains under the overall cap).
Tool_tax_saving_fd_ExampleStep_5: Interest earned over 5 years is taxable as per your slab.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_Step_2: Choose annual or quarterly compounding for 5 years.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_Step_4: Estimate 80C tax saving as eligible × slab.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_Info_Purpose: Estimate maturity and 80C tax saving on a 5-year tax-saver FD.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_Info_WhyKnow: Lock-in is fixed at 5 years; compounding and slab decide returns and tax benefit.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_Info_RealLife: Using leftover 80C room at your bank, comparing with ELSS and PPF.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_Info_Goal_3: Estimate tax saving at your slab
Tool_tax_saving_fd_Info_Alt_Elss_When: When you accept market risk for potentially higher returns.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_Info_Alt_Fd_When: When you need flexible tenure without 80C.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra combines 5-year lock-in maturity, compounding choice, and 80C tax-saving estimate in one tool.
Tool_tax_saving_fd_Info_Motto: Lock in for five years — claim 80C with clarity.
